![horizontal line][image1]

**Sotatek JSC**  
Vietnam

## AI Jewelry Design Assistant — Proof of Concept

## **9th Jul 2026**

# **OVERVIEW**

The objective is to develop an AI chatbot that enables a Hong Kong-based jewelry designer to create new jewelry concepts through natural language prompts and image uploads.

By streamlining the ideation and design process, the solution aims to reduce reliance on external freelancers, accelerate design cycles, and empower the user to explore and realize creative ideas more independently.

The chatbot supports four stages of the design pipeline:

1. **Ideation** — moodboards, concepts, and trend references generated from a prompt  
2. **Sketching (2D)** — refined sketch variations of a selected concept direction  
3. **Rendering** — photorealistic renders across metals and angles  
4. **3D Modeling** — an inspectable, exportable 3D file for the final selected design

# **SCOPE OF WORK**

### **In Scope**

* AI chatbot for the jewelry product design pipeline: moodboard generation, AI-generated visual renderings, 3D model generation and export, prompt- and image-based interaction  
* Deployment on the customer's own AWS account

  ### **Out of Scope**

* Production-ready infra  
* Technical CAD file generation  
* Manufacturing-ready designs

**Note on the .step export item in Section 4:** the client has explicitly excluded "Technical CAD file generation" above. The .step-compatible export described in Section 4.3 is a **format-compatibility convenience only** (see limitations there) — it is **not** CAD generation capability and does not change the exclusion above. It is presented as an optional add-on for discussion, not assumed into scope by default.

### **Assumptions, Dependencies & Limitations**

| Type | Item |
| ----- | ----- |
| Dependency | Amazon Bedrock model availability (Claude, Stability AI) confirmed for the target AWS region; latency acceptable for Hong Kong-based users |
| Dependency | EC2 GPU instance size (e.g. G5 family variant) confirmed against expected concurrency and render/3D turnaround targets |
| Assumption | Generated assets (renders, 3D files) are persisted to Amazon S3 rather than local instance storage |
| Assumption | Bedrock integration defaults to the current generally-available Claude Sonnet-tier model rather than a fixed version, to avoid deprecation risk |
| Assumption | Exact user flows are finalized during requirements workshops in the first sprint |
| Limitation | 3D output is a **mesh** (.stl/.obj) — the native output of image-to-3D generation, suitable for visualization/preview, not manufacturing |
| Limitation | The optional .step export (Section 4.3) is a facet-based wrapper, not parametric/NURBS CAD — see explicit limitations before treating it as production-ready |
| Dependency | Project management is provided by SoftwareOne; SotaTek's team covers solution architecture, business analysis, and engineering delivery only |
| Assumption | AWS infrastructure and model token costs are covered separately by the customer's AWS credits, and are excluded from the fees in Section 5 |
| Preference (not mandatory) | Hong Kong-based, Cantonese-speaking team members, where available |

# **TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE & MECHANISM**

### **Architecture Flow**

![][image2]

### **Pipeline Stage → Service Mapping**

| Stage | Function | Underlying Service |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Ideation | Prompt interpretation, moodboard/concept generation | Bedrock: Claude (orchestration) \+ Stability AI (imagery) |
| Sketching | 2D sketch variation generation | Bedrock: Stability AI, orchestrated via Claude |
| Rendering | Photorealistic multi-angle/multi-metal renders | Bedrock: Stability AI (higher-fidelity render pass) |
| 3D Modeling | Converts a selected render into an exportable mesh | Trellis (image-to-3D), running on the EC2 GPU instance |

### **3D Export Mechanism — Mesh (.stl) and Optional CAD-Compatible (.step)**

**Core mechanism (.stl — in scope):** Trellis generates a triangle mesh directly from the selected 2D render. This is the native, reliable output of current image-to-3D AI models and is included as the standard 3D deliverable.

**Optional add-on mechanism (.step):** for cases where the design team wants to preview the model inside standard CAD software (SolidWorks, Rhino, Fusion 360, FreeCAD, Inventor, Matrix/RhinoGold) rather than a dedicated mesh viewer, the mesh can be wrapped into a STEP container using OpenCASCADE (via the cadquery/OCC Python bindings):

1. The mesh's triangles are reconstructed into faceted B-rep faces  
2. Adjacent faces are "sewn" into closed shells  
3. The result is written out per the ISO-10303-21 (AUTOMOTIVE\_DESIGN schema) STEP standard  
4. The output file is independently re-validated in a separate process to confirm it opens correctly (avoiding false negatives from memory limits during generation)

**Explicit limitations of the .step output — must be communicated to the client if this option is selected:**

* It is a **facet-based wrapper** of the original mesh — it does **not** contain true NURBS/parametric surfaces, and cannot be edited the way a native CAD model can (no curve/edge control)  
* Geometry is a **single fused solid** — individual components (e.g. stone, prongs, band) are not separated into distinct, independently editable solids  
* **No manufacturing tolerances** are applied (no casting shrinkage allowance, no wall-thickness validation)  
* **Not production/manufacturing-ready** — a CAD designer still needs to model the piece natively (e.g. in Rhino/Matrix/JewelCAD) using this file only as a visual reference

**Recommendation:** position this strictly as *"a CAD-software-compatible reference export to speed up the hand-off to a CAD designer — not a replacement for CAD modeling."* This keeps it consistent with the client's own exclusion of CAD generation from scope.

# **EPIC & TASK BREAKDOWN**

| Epic | Task | Role |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 1\. Discovery & Architecture Validation | Facilitate requirements workshops with client | BA |
|  | Validate proposed AWS architecture (Section 4\) | SA |
|  | Confirm Bedrock model/region availability for Hong Kong | SA |
|  | Define S3 storage structure for generated assets | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | Document detailed chatbot workflows & functional spec | BA |
| 2\. Chatbot Core & Orchestration | Set up Streamlit chatbot UI on the EC2 instance | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | Configure Claude on Bedrock for prompt orchestration | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | Implement conversation/session state handling | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | Configure CloudFront CDN & encryption | Cloud/AI Engineer |
| 3\. Ideation, Sketching & Rendering Pipeline | Integrate Stability AI for moodboard/concept generation | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | Implement 2D sketch variation flow | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | Implement photorealistic multi-metal/angle rendering flow | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | Tune prompts for jewelry-specific outputs | BA \+ Cloud/AI Engineer |
| 4\. 3D Modeling & Export | Deploy Trellis (image-to-3D) on the EC2 GPU instance | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | Implement .stl mesh export & persistence to S3 | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | In-app 3D preview (inspect, rotate, check dimensions) | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | *(Optional)* Mesh-to-STEP export pipeline — see Section 4.3 | Cloud/AI Engineer |
| 5\. Infrastructure & Security | Provision & size the EC2 GPU instance | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | Configure IAM role scoping for Bedrock/S3 access | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | Set up S3 buckets and access policy | Cloud/AI Engineer |
| 6\. Testing & UAT | Prepare test cases across all 4 pipeline stages | Cloud/AI Engineer |
|  | Support client UAT sessions | Cloud/AI Engineer (UAT Support) |
|  | Triage and fix bugs identified during UAT | Cloud/AI Engineer |
| 7\. Documentation & Handover | Prepare handover documentation | Cloud/AI Engineer |

# **TEAM STRUCTURE, SCHEDULE & COST**

### **Team Structure**

* Pre-Sales / Solution Architect x1 (part-time)  
* Consultant / Business Analyst x1 (part-time)  
* Cloud / AI Engineer x1  
* UAT Support (up to 2 days within the UAT period)

### **Schedule**

| Phase | Duration |
| ----- | ----- |
| Design, build & configuration | 6–7 weeks |
| User Acceptance Testing (UAT) | 2 weeks (incl. up to 2 days active support) |
| PoC Closure (docs, KT, AWS handover) | within the above |

# **DELIVERABLES**

The items will be available for the developed version:

- Functional AI chatbot covering Ideation, Sketching, Rendering, and 3D Modeling  
- Deployed solution on the customer's AWS account (EC2, Bedrock, CloudFront, S3)  
- 3D file export in .stl format  
- Source code repository  
- Test cases and UAT test report  
- Technical documentation and knowledge transfer
